subject: SOUTH AFRICA
assessment: South Africa faces challenges related to corruption, inequality, and racial tensions, but its democratic institutions and constitution provide a foundation for stability. The ruling African National Congress (ANC) has been in power since the end of apartheid, with current President Cyril Ramaphosa navigating internal party conflicts and external economic pressures. International relations are balanced between African solidarity, BRICS membership, and Western partnerships, posing both opportunities and risks.
